evals.prompt: I just joined a team and need to get up to speed on this repo. What should I know before I start making changes?
evals.expected_output: A structured report covering file churn hotspots, contributor bus factor, bug-prone files, project momentum, and firefighting patterns. Should connect the dots between sections.

assertions.text: Report includes file churn analysis identifying the most frequently changed files
Report discusses contributor distribution and bus factor risk
Report identifies bug-prone files by analyzing fix/bug-related commits
Report includes project activity/momentum over time
Report discusses revert/hotfix/emergency patterns
Report synthesizes findings across sections rather than just dumping raw data
Report includes actionable recommendations for the user

evals.expected_output: Same structured report as above, triggered by direct invocation. Should orient first (README, repo size, age, language), then run all analysis steps.

evals.prompt: Can you give me a health check of this codebase? I want to know where the risk areas are before I start refactoring.
evals.expected_output: A report with emphasis on risk areas — high-churn files, bug clusters, and the overlap between them. Should include actionable recommendations for where to be careful when refactoring.

assertions.text: Report includes file churn analysis identifying the most frequently changed files
Report discusses contributor distribution and bus factor risk
Report identifies bug-prone files by analyzing fix/bug-related commits
Report includes project activity/momentum over time
Report discusses revert/hotfix/emergency patterns
Report synthesizes findings across sections rather than just dumping raw data
Report includes actionable recommendations for the user
